disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid, pounding, bounding heartbeat that comes on with standing and eases on lying down
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me more about the pounding heart — when does it happen?
What is your heartbeat doing during these episodes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's like my heart is trying to jump out of my chest — it goes really fast and hard, mostly when I'm up on my feet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Generalised coarse/fine tremor and shaking during episodes
trigger_question_examples: Tell me about the shaking — where is it and when does it happen?
Is the trembling all over or just your hands?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My hands and my whole body shake, like I've had ten coffees. It's worst when the racing heart hits.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Presyncope — lightheadedness and a feeling of being about to pass out on standing (no actual loss of consciousness)
trigger_question_examples: When you stand, do you feel dizzy or like you might faint?
Do you get lightheaded when you get up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, the moment I stand I feel like I'm going to black out — like my head is swimming and I have to grab something.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms are strictly posture-dependent — provoked within seconds of standing and reliably relieved by lying flat
trigger_question_examples: Does lying down flat make the racing heart and dizziness settle?
Is it specifically standing up that brings this on, and does it go when you lie back down?
Do the symptoms happen at rest lying down, or only when you are upright?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: That's exactly it — flat on my back I'm okay, my heart calms down. The second I stand it all kicks off again. That's why I'm too scared to get up.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't know, it just happens.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Intense adrenaline-surge sensation with secondary panic/anxiety during episodes
trigger_question_examples: How do you feel in yourself when it happens — any sense of panic or a rush?
Do you get an adrenaline or fight-or-flight feeling with it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's this horrible flood of adrenaline and dread, like something awful is about to happen. I do panic, but it starts with the physical stuff.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known long-standing autonomic condition — prior similar milder episodes; previously told the nervous system fails to control heart rate/BP on standing
trigger_question_examples: Has this happened before, and were you ever given a name for it?
Do you have any known heart or nervous-system condition?
Have you seen a specialist about these episodes in the past?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, it's happened on and off for a couple of years, just never this bad. A specialist told me my nervous system doesn't control my heart rate and blood pressure right when I stand up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Red-flag screen NEGATIVE: no chest pain, no actual fainting/collapse (presyncope only), no fever
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any crushing or heavy chest pain with this?
Have you actually passed out or collapsed, or just felt like you might?
Have you had any fever or felt hot and unwell in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No crushing chest pain — it's more pounding than painful. I haven't actually blacked out, just felt like I would. And no fever, I checked, it was 36.8.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Structured supine-vs-standing home readings on request: HR ~75 supine rising to ~140 standing; BP ~120/80 supine rising to ~155/100 standing (patient-obtained, unverified)
trigger_question_examples: If you have a home BP monitor, can you lie down for 5 minutes then stand and check your blood pressure and heart rate?
Can you compare your heart rate and blood pressure lying down versus standing?
Do you have any home readings of your blood pressure in both positions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I borrowed a blood pressure monitor. Lying down it was about 120 over 80 and my watch said 75. When I stood up it went to about 155 over 100 and my heart rate shot up to around 140.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't have a blood pressure machine at home.
